title: Timestamp column won't accept insert without time
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE testdate (id INTEGER NOT NULL, adate DATE, adatetime DATETIME, PRIMARY KEY (id));
INSERT INTO testdate (id, adate, adatetime) VALUES (1, '2007-10-30', '2007-10-30 12:00:00');
INSERT INTO testdate (id, adate, adatetime) VALUES (2, '2007-10-30', NULL);
INSERT INTO testdate (id, adate, adatetime) VALUES (3, '2007-10-30', '2007-10-30 23:59:59');
SELECT * FROM testdate WHERE adatetime IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Timestamp column requires time for successful insertion
